title: CastingCompass consented first-party trip log

access.reason: Trip evidence is private first-party data. Only a minimized, consented, deletion-checked validation envelope may leave the production data store.
instructions: Export only records admitted by the frozen validation protocol; a structurally valid trip is not automatically model or evaluation evidence.
Derive participant_group_id with the approved privacy-safe procedure and never export an account ID, email address, reporter key or reporter-key hash.
Exclude notes, photos, precise coordinates and other free text from the validation envelope.
Preserve authoritative site-window scoring identity, pre-outcome selection and score-influence context, consent state, evidence status and deletion lineage.
Keep retrospective, preactivation, score-visible, context-only and unverified records outside the primary benchmark.
access.ingestion_command: python -m pipeline.contourcast.cli seal-validation-splits --protocol validation/protocols/california-halibut-site-window-v1.json --label-free-evidence PRIVATE_LABEL_FREE_EVIDENCE.jsonl --opportunity-ledger PRIVATE_OPPORTUNITY_LEDGER.json --predictions PRIVATE_CANDIDATE_PREDICTIONS.json --manifest-chain PRIVATE_ACTIVATION.json --output PRIVATE_SPLIT_MANIFEST.json

limitations: Self-selected and score-influenced trips are subject to selection bias and are not primary validation evidence.
A no-catch trip is a target non-encounter during declared effort, not proof that California halibut were biologically absent.
Curated site support can substantiate only site-by-time-window ranking claims, never precise casting-zone claims.
Evidence remains subject to consent withdrawal, account deletion and validation-protocol admission rules.
